original_abstract: We present Video-LLaMA, a multi-modal framework that empowers Large Language Models (LLMs) with the capability of understanding both visual and auditory content in the video. Video-LLaMA bootstraps cross-modal training from the frozen pre-trained visual & audio encoders and the frozen LLMs. Unlike previous works that complement LLMs to process the visual or audio signals only, Video-LLaMA enables video comprehension by tackling two challenges: (1) capturing the temporal changes in visual scenes, (2) integrating audio-visual signals. To counter the first challenge, we propose a Video Q-former to assemble a pre-trained image encoder into our video encoder and introduce a video-to-text generation task to learn video-language correspondence. For the second challenge, we leverage ImageBind, a universal embedding model aligning multiple modalities, as the pre-trained audio encoder and introduce an Audio Q-former on top of ImageBind to learn reasonable auditory query embeddings for the LLM module. To align the output of both visual & audio encoders with LLM’s embedding space, we first train Video-LLaMA on massive video/image-caption pairs and then tune our model with visual-instruction datasets of moderate amount but higher quality. We found Video-LLaMA shows the ability to perceive and comprehend video content and generate meaningful responses grounded in the visual and auditory information presented in the videos.
humanized_abstract: Video-LLaMA: Exploiting Visual and Audio Cues with Large Language Models We introduce Video-LLaMA, a multi-modal framework that leverages both the visual and audio cues present in a video to assist Large Language Models (LLMs) in grounding their outputs to the actual content shown in a video. Video-LLaMA starts by bootstrapping cross-modal training from the frozen pre-trained visual and audio features and the frozen LLMs. The main contribution of Video-LLaMA is to address the two major hurdles in grounding LLMs to videos: (1) modelling temporal visual structures and (2) integrating audio-visual modalities. To address the first hurdle, the Video Q-former is proposed to transform a pre-trained image encoder into a video encoder. The video-to-text generation task is introduced to learn the video-language correspondences. To address the second hurdle, the ImageBind (IB) is adopted as the pre-trained audio encoder. The Audio Q-former is further proposed to learn reasonable audio query embeddings for the LLM module. To match the visual/audio features to the embedding space of the LLM, we pre-train Video-LLaMA with the massive video/image-caption pairs and fine-tune Video-LLaMA with moderate amounts of visual-instruction datasets with higher quality. Experimental results demonstrate the capacity of Video-LLaMA in sensing the video content and generating output responses that are grounded on the visual and audio cues within the videos.

The video-to-text generation task is introduced to learn the video-language correspondences. To address the second hurdle, the ImageBind (IB) is adopted as the pre-trained audio encoder. To match the visual/audio features to the embedding space of the LLM, we pre-train Video-LLaMA with the massive video/image-caption pairs and fine-tune Video-LLaMA with moderate amounts of visual-instruction datasets with higher quality.
